slides.headline: 20 applications. 6 vetted.
slides.eyebrow: Proof · The vetting milestone — detailed feedback
fit_warnings: [01 hook.eyebrow] copy (49 chars) exceeds slot budget (48 at 44px) — needs a rewrite

slides.body: Here's the detailed feedback we gave the 14 who didn't make it — because rejection without explanation is useless. ↓
The 14 we didn't accept were rejected for readiness, not music.

/14 had rights issues
→ Labels holding masters with no clear route to clearance
→ Uncleared samples embedded in commercial-quality work
→ Publishing registered with the wrong entity /14 had metadata problems
→ No ISRC codes assigned
→ No BPM, key, or mood data
→ Files with no embedded information /14 had file issues
→ Only MP3 available
→ No stems exported or accessible
→ Files not named to any standard /14 had response issues
→ 3+ day reply time in the response-time test scenario Most of these overlap. Most are fixable. → What each rejection reason actually means
→ How to fix rights issues (and what takes longest)
→ The metadata checklist you need on every file
→ File format requirements for sync
→ How to build a faster response culture Come back when you've fixed it. The application stays open. #SyncReady #AfricanComposers #SyncMaster #MusicBusiness #MusicRights
